comments.author: @Pleiades_Virgo
comments.text: Dear Adnan, I love your videos but couldn't help asking this question after seeing the India one. Have you enhanced this video with the help of AI? I have to ask this, as someone who fairly frequently visits various parts of India, because you've got in there a starry night sky against which a lit-up Qutub Minar is shining. That the Minar is shown to be lit after nightfall is not in itself a problem. What certainly is one, however, is the black sky with the stars in the backdrop. Delhi is an outrageously polluted city, and I've never once, regardless of season, in my 24 years of taking regular trips to India, beheld a blue or clear sky there. There's a permanent, thick grey layer of airborne gaseous refuse hanging over the National Capital Region (NCR) that blocks astral bodies from being seen practically all the time. A clear black night sky with lots of visible stars over the Indian capital is quite simply not something that's within the realm of possibility. So, I end my comment by humbly querying, again, whether you've modified at least parts of the footage with AI. Thank you.
